data-next-head="">{"@context":{"@vocab":"http://schema.org","articleId":{"@id":"Text","@type":"@id"},"topics":{"@id":"Text","@type":"@id"}},"@type":"NewsArticle","headline":"Europe celebrates as Orbán's stunning defeat deals a blow to Putin and Trump","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/world/europe/europe-celebrates-orban-defeat-hungary-election-putin-trump-maga-rcna331478","thumbnailUrl":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_social_share_1024x768_scale,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-04/260413-Peter-Magyar-rs-346368.jpg","alternativeHeadline":"Europe celebrates as Orbán's stunning defeat deals a blow to Putin and Trump","articleBody":"LONDON —  “Europe! Europe!” was the chant ringing out from the banks of the Danube after an election upset that was celebrated across the continent Monday as a stunning defeat for those threatening its security. Presidents, prime ministers and European Union officials hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity and shared values in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.   Orbán, a close ally of Russia's Vladimir Putin and President Donald Trump, is viewed as a trailblazer for the global hard right. He conceded defeat Sunday night in what turned out to be a sweeping victory for challenger Peter Magyar and the center-right Tisza Party, which successfully united the country's opposition behind a vow to restore ties with Europe. Tisza was set to win 138 seats in the 199-seat parliament, with almost all votes counted. In a news conference Monday, Magyar offered pragmatic support for the United Nations and the European Union, a huge shift in Hungary's position towards Brussels. \"We are not going there to fight for the sake of fighting so we can write on billboards that Brussels is evil and needs to be stopped,\" he told supporters in a direct swipe at Orbán's negative campaigning, which also painted Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a potential enemy. Magyar, who added that it was clear that Ukraine was the \"victim\" in the war, also accused the outgoing government of shredding documents relating to sanctions against Russia, citing an \"insider\" at the foreign ministry.  A succession of leaders called Magyar to offer their congratulations even before he took to the stage to address large crowds in the capital, Budapest, to declare victory. Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk wrote on X: “Hungary, Poland, Europe, Back together! Glorious victory, dear friends!” He added “Ruszkik haza,” meaning “Russians go home” in Hungarian, a reference to Orban's backing for Putin and repeated veto on European support for Ukraine against Russia's invasion. In a video clip posted by Tusk, the clearly delighted Polish leader is seen speaking to Magyar by phone and says \"I think I'm happier than you.\" Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke for many on the continent when he posted: \"Today Europe wins and European values win.\" The European Democratic Party, a pan-European centrist group that supports greater integration across the continent, called Magyar's victory \"a cry that shakes the conscience of us all\" and posted a clip of crowds chanting \"Europe! Europe!\" at his victory rally in Budapest. Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain — no longer a member of the E.U. but a close supporter of Ukraine — said: \"This is an historic moment, not only for Hungary, but for European democracy.\" The Kremlin, for its part, said Monday it hoped to continue its \"pragmatic\" ties with Hungary. “The Hungarians have made their choice. We respect this choice,\" spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. But asked later whether the Kremlin would congratulate Magyar, Peskov said “We don’t extend greetings to unfriendly countries. And Hungary is an unfriendly country.\" Those celebrating the fall of Orbán should be mindful, analysts cautioned, that the huge influence he and his supporters built across the country’s institutions will not be easily removed. “The structure of the last 16 years has been so entrenched and so much concerned with capturing all the commanding heights of the Hungarian state that it will take quite some time for anyone to unravel this,” said Jonathan Eyal, associated director of the Royal United Services Institute, a think tank in London. Orbán, 62, is a key European ally of Trump whose populism and culture war posturing has provided a model for the MAGA movement.  In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces. The now former Hungarian leader has been widely criticized for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists. Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. \"We will restore the system of checks and balances,\" Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday.  He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been halted due to repeated infringements and concerns about corruption and the democratic process. But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists who have taken power away from Hungary's parliament to make policy, Eyal pointed out, as well as a powerful constitutional court full of his appointees. \"So sweeping away all these cobwebs is not something that could happen after a party on the Danube River,\" Eyal warned. Magyar, a relatively young leader at 45, is a former member of Orbán's Fidesz Party and a former official in the Foreign Ministry — he only broke away to join Tisza in 2024. Magyar is a traditionalist who support the hardline stance of the previous administration on immigration and has even signaled he may go further, by scrapping the country's guest worker scheme for non-E.U. citizens. \"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader,\" Eyal said. \"So there is, there is a legitimate question about how much of a break this is from someone who clearly shared some of the Orbán government's views for a lot of his political career.\" And there is unlikely to be a complete severing of ties with Moscow. At his press conference on Monday, Magyar said he hoped the war in Ukraine would soon end so international sanctions on Russia can be lifted — Hungary is a major importer of Russian oil. Hungary will try to diversify, he said, but added that the country can't \"change geography.\"  Whatever happens, this result is blow to the regime in Moscow, Eyal added. \"Most Hungarian voters when they're asked, they say we don't want to be part of Russia, we want to be part of Europe,\" he said. \"And so this is a very major lesson for far right.\"","dateModified":"2026-04-13T13:46:17.207Z","datePublished":"2026-04-13T11:28:06.034Z","articleSection":"world","articleId":"rcna331478","identifier":{"@type":"PropertyValue","propertyID":"uid","value":"rcna331478"},"topics":"","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-04/260413-Peter-Magyar-rs-346368.jpg","caption":"Peter Magyar, leader of the opposition Tisza party, waves the Hungarian flag following the announcement of the partial results of the parliamentary election, in Budapest, Hungary, Sunday, April 12, 2026. ","height":1666,"width":2500},"author":[{"@context":"https://schema.org/","@type":"Person","name":"Patrick Smith","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,f_auto,q_auto:best/newscms/2026_05/3717940/250127_1x1_nbc_headshots_patrick_smith_5389.jpg","jobTitle":"Breaking News Editor / Reporter","description":"Patrick Smith is a London-based editor and reporter for NBC News Digital.","disambiguatingDescription":"I spent five years on the world team, writing about news and politics across Europe and beyond. I focused on the growth of extremism and the polarization of national and international political movements — including in-depth articles on the Netherlands  Hungary, Germany, Italy as well as examining the growth of global far-right terrorism. I spent five years before that as a reporter and editor at BuzzFeed News in London, where I wrote about all manner of internet culture, crime, conspiracy theories and the U.K.'s housing crisis. I previously worked in B2B media, where I wrote about the future of the media industry, and spent some time as a journalism lecturer. I've reported on two presidential elections, four British general elections, one Scottish referendum, one Brexit referendum, and many court cases. I studied history at university and take a personal interest in stories relating to ancient and medieval history and archeology.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/patrick-smith-ncpn989401","sameAs":["https://x.com/psmith"]}],"publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC Europe!” was the chant ringing out from the banks of the Danube<a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/world/hungary/hungary-parliamentary-election-results-rcna273661" target="_blank"> after an election upset </a>that was celebrated across the continent Monday as a stunning defeat for those threatening its security.</p><button class="styles_subscriptionCTA__LOUeY" id="subscriptionCTA" type="button"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," alt="NBC News Icon" class="styles_icon__wRwWv" aria-hidden="true"/><div class="styles_textWrapper__vxvko"><p class="styles_text__daaVB styles_textLarge__qlzwu">Subscribe to read this story ad-free    </p><p class="styles_text__daaVB">Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.</p></div><img src="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/rockcms/2026-01/arrow-f55fa5.svg" alt="arrow" class="styles_arrow__tnruW" aria-hidden="true"/></button><p id="anchor-4dd9b7" class="body-graf">Presidents, prime ministers and European Union officials hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity and shared values in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.  </p><div class="ad dn-print ad--text-alignment ad-container boxinlineAd" data-testid="ad__container"><div data-mps="true" data-slot="boxinline" data-sizes="[[[1000,1],[]],[[758,1],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[728,90],[360,360]]],[[0,0],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[360,360]]]]" data-targeting="{}" data-render-on-view="true" data-active-tab="true" data-offset-viewport="100" data-testid="ad__container__inner"></div></div><div id="taboolaReadMoreBelow"></div><p id="anchor-4b299f" class="body-graf">Orbán, <a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/world/hungary/16-years-power-putins-closest-friend-europe-faces-pivotal-election-rcna273664" target="_blank">a close ally of Russia's Vladimir Putin</a> and President Donald Trump, is viewed as a trailblazer for the global hard right. He conceded defeat Sunday night in what turned out to be a sweeping victory for challenger Peter Magyar and the center-right Tisza Party, which successfully united the country's opposition behind a vow to restore ties with Europe.</p><section class="inline-video inline-video--in-body inline-video--vertical-video" data-testid="inline-video" id="anchor-ac6b8f"><div class="inline-video__background"><div class="inline-video__player"><div tabindex="-1"></div><div class="inline-video__badge-wrapper"><div class="subscriber-badge styles_subscriberBadge__Lc_nV" data-testid="subscriber-badge"><img src="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/rockcms/2026-01/asterisk-43f04b.svg" alt=""/><div class="styles_textWrapper__6GGIR"><span class="styles_text__CtcQ0">FOR</span><span class="styles_text__CtcQ0">SUBSCRIBERS</span></div></div></div><button type="button" class="inline-video-gate" tabindex="0" aria-label="Subscribe to watch this video"></button><img src="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-360w,f_avif,q_auto:eco,dpr_2/mpx/2704722219/2026_04/alexthumb3-r9i45b.jpg" alt="Why Hungary's election matters far beyond its borders" class="inline-video__thumbnail"/><div class="inline-video__placeholder-controls"><div class="styles_controlContainer__XmNB0 styles_inlineVideo__unIwH" role="region" aria-label="Video controls"><div class="styles_controlSidebar__yyieA"><div class="styles_hide__7Ii73"><button type="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Close video" class="styles_controlBtn__JvHu1" data-testid="video-icon-button-close"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," alt="" role="presentation"/></button></div><div aria-hidden="true" class="styles_hide__7Ii73"></div><div class="styles_hide__7Ii73"><button type="button" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1" aria-label="Tap to play video" class="styles_controlBtn__JvHu1" data-testid="video-icon-button-play"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMTUiIGhlaWdodD0iMTUiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PHBhdGggZD0iTTAgMHYxNWwxNS03LjQxeiIgZmlsbD0iI0ZGRiIgZmlsbC1ydWxlPSJldmVub2RkIi8+PC9zdmc+" alt="" role="presentation"/></button><button type="button" aria-hidden="false" tabindex="0" aria-label="Tap to mute video" class="styles_controlBtn__JvHu1 undefined" data-testid="video-icon-button-volume"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," alt="" role="presentation"/></button></div></div><p class="styles_title__0GjQf styles_inlineVideoInitial__c2yet"></p><span class="styles_gradientOverlay__6My_d"></span><div class="styles_controlGroupTime__HOSin styles_hide__7Ii73"><span aria-label="Elapsed time 00:00">00:00</span><span aria-label="Remaining time 00:00">00:00</span></div></div></div><div class="inline-video__placeholder-info"><h2 class="inline-video__placeholder-title">Why Hungary's election matters far beyond its borders</h2><div class="styles_videoMeta__IHKZz"><span class="styles_caret__Aqe2z icon icon-play" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" data-testid="caret"></span><span data-testid="card-duration">01:48</span></div></div></div></div></section><p id="anchor-29db29" class="body-graf">Tisza was set to win 138 seats in the 199-seat parliament, with almost all votes counted.</p><p id="anchor-cbd468" class="body-graf">In a news conference Monday, Magyar offered pragmatic support for the United Nations and the European Union, a huge shift in Hungary's position towards Brussels.</p><p id="anchor-49ce33" class="body-graf">"We are not going there to fight for the sake of fighting so we can write on billboards that Brussels is evil and needs to be stopped," he told supporters in a direct swipe at Orbán's negative campaigning, which also painted Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a potential enemy.</p><div class="ad dn-print ad--text-alignment ad-container boxinlineAd" data-testid="ad__container"><div data-mps="true" data-slot="boxinline" data-sizes="[[[1000,1],[]],[[758,1],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[728,90],[360,360]]],[[0,0],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[360,360]]]]" data-targeting="{}" data-render-on-view="true" data-active-tab="true" data-offset-viewport="100" data-testid="ad__container__inner"></div></div><p id="anchor-bfc874" class="body-graf">Magyar, who added that it was clear that Ukraine was the "victim" in the war, also accused the outgoing government of shredding documents relating to sanctions against Russia, citing an "insider" at the foreign ministry. </p><p id="anchor-f3fd76" class="body-graf">A succession of leaders called Magyar to offer their congratulations even before he took to the stage to address large crowds in the capital, Budapest, to declare victory.</p><p id="anchor-04853f" class="body-graf">Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk wrote on X: “Hungary, Poland, Europe, Back together! Glorious victory, dear friends!” He added “Ruszkik haza,” meaning “Russians go home” in Hungarian, a reference to Orban's backing for Putin and repeated veto on European support for Ukraine against Russia's invasion.</p><p id="anchor-d07d20" class="body-graf">In a video clip posted by Tusk, the clearly delighted Polish leader is seen speaking to Magyar by phone and says "I think I'm happier than you."</p><p id="anchor-ec704c" class="body-graf">Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke for many on the continent when he posted: "Today Europe wins and European values win."</p><p id="anchor-ad7bfd" class="body-graf">The European Democratic Party, a pan-European centrist group that supports greater integration across the continent, called Magyar's victory "a cry that shakes the conscience of us all" and <a href="https://x.com/democrats_eu/status/2043442306960036307" target="_blank">posted a clip</a> of crowds chanting "Europe! Europe!" at his victory rally in Budapest.</p><div class="ad dn-print ad--text-alignment ad-container boxinlineAd" data-testid="ad__container"><div data-mps="true" data-slot="boxinline" data-sizes="[[[1000,1],[]],[[758,1],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[728,90],[360,360]]],[[0,0],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[360,360]]]]" data-targeting="{}" data-render-on-view="true" data-active-tab="true" data-offset-viewport="100" data-testid="ad__container__inner"></div></div><p id="anchor-d640b1" class="body-graf">Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain — no longer a member of the E.U. but a close supporter of Ukraine — said: "This is an historic moment, not only for Hungary, but for European democracy."</p><p id="anchor-b84410" class="body-graf">The Kremlin, for its part, said Monday it hoped to continue its "pragmatic" ties with Hungary. “The Hungarians have made their choice. We respect this choice," spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.</p><p id="anchor-e036bd" class="body-graf">But asked later whether the Kremlin would congratulate Magyar, Peskov said “We don’t extend greetings to unfriendly countries. And Hungary is an unfriendly country."</p><figure class="styles_inlineImage__FvnTh styles_medium__MEKii" id="anchor-9d09b9"><picture class="styles_image__i32F7" data-testid="picture" data-flavor="fit" data-original-height="1667" data-original-width="2500"><source media="(min-width: 1000px)" srcSet="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-560w,f_avif,q_auto:eco,dpr_2/rockcms/2026-04/260413-hungary-rs-0802c3.jpg 2x, https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-560w,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-04/260413-hungary-rs-0802c3.jpg 1x"/><source media="(min-width: 320px)" srcSet="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-760w,f_avif,q_auto:eco,dpr_2/rockcms/2026-04/260413-hungary-rs-0802c3.jpg 2x, https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-760w,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-04/260413-hungary-rs-0802c3.jpg 1x"/><img loading="lazy" src="https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-760w,f_auto,q_auto:best/rockcms/2026-04/260413-hungary-rs-0802c3.jpg" alt="Hungary Holds Parliamentary Elections" height="1667" width="2500"/></picture><figcaption class="caption styles_caption__TCewG" data-testid="caption"><span class="caption__container" data-testid="caption__container">Supporters celebrate the resounding Tisza party win in Hungarian parliamentary elections Sunday in Budapest. </span><span class="caption__source" data-testid="caption__source">Sean Gallup / Getty Images</span></figcaption></figure><p id="anchor-3bb267" class="body-graf">Those celebrating the fall of Orbán should be mindful, analysts cautioned, that the huge influence he and his supporters built across the country’s institutions will not be easily removed.</p><p id="anchor-2a6575" class="body-graf">“The structure of the last 16 years has been so entrenched and so much concerned with capturing all the commanding heights of the Hungarian state that it will take quite some time for anyone to unravel this,” said Jonathan Eyal, associated director of the Royal United Services Institute, a think tank in London.</p><p id="anchor-b07b58" class="body-graf">Orbán, 62, is a key European ally of Trump whose populism and culture war posturing has <a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/viktor-orban-cpac-trump-gop-hungary-leader-rcna40199" target="_blank">provided a model for the MAGA movement</a>. </p><div class="ad dn-print ad--text-alignment ad-container boxinlineAd" data-testid="ad__container"><div data-mps="true" data-slot="boxinline" data-sizes="[[[1000,1],[]],[[758,1],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[728,90],[360,360]]],[[0,0],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[360,360]]]]" data-targeting="{}" data-render-on-view="true" data-active-tab="true" data-offset-viewport="100" data-testid="ad__container__inner"></div></div><p id="anchor-44dc58" class="body-graf">In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces.</p><p id="anchor-54e593" class="body-graf">The now former Hungarian leader has been<a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/viktor-orban-cpac-trump-gop-hungary-leader-rcna40199" target="_blank"> widely criticized</a> for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists.</p><p id="anchor-66f141" class="body-graf">Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. "We will restore the system of checks and balances," Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday. </p><p id="anchor-a5c9bd" class="body-graf">He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been <a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/european-union-battling-poland-hungary-illiberal-policies-rcna2888" target="_blank">halted due to repeated infringements </a>and concerns about corruption and the democratic process.</p><p id="anchor-df2b45" class="body-graf">But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists who have taken power away from Hungary's parliament to make policy, Eyal pointed out, as well as a powerful constitutional court full of his appointees.</p><p id="anchor-632381" class="body-graf">"So sweeping away all these cobwebs is not something that could happen after a party on the Danube River," Eyal warned.</p><div class="ad dn-print ad--text-alignment ad-container boxinlineAd" data-testid="ad__container"><div data-mps="true" data-slot="boxinline" data-sizes="[[[1000,1],[]],[[758,1],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[728,90],[360,360]]],[[0,0],[[300,250],[700,50],[5,5],[360,360]]]]" data-targeting="{}" data-render-on-view="true" data-active-tab="true" data-offset-viewport="100" data-testid="ad__container__inner"></div></div><p id="anchor-0e4b5e" class="body-graf">Magyar, a relatively young leader at 45, is a former member of Orbán's Fidesz Party and a former official in the Foreign Ministry — he only broke away to join Tisza in 2024.</p><p id="anchor-5277d0" class="body-graf">Magyar is a traditionalist who support the hardline stance of the previous administration on immigration and has even signaled he may go further, by scrapping the country's guest worker scheme for non-E.U. citizens.</p><p id="anchor-c2c580" class="body-graf">"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader," Eyal said. "So there is, there is a legitimate question about how much of a break this is from someone who clearly shared some of the Orbán government's views for a lot of his political career."</p><p id="anchor-cb33a1" class="body-graf">And there is unlikely to be a complete severing of ties with Moscow. At his press conference on Monday, Magyar said he hoped the war in Ukraine would soon end so international sanctions on Russia can be lifted — Hungary is a major importer of Russian oil. Hungary will try to diversify, he said, but added that the country can't "change geography." </p><p id="anchor-e9987f" class="body-graf">Whatever happens, this result is blow to the regime in Moscow, Eyal added.</p><p id="anchor-4f6242" class="endmark body-graf">"Most Hungarian voters when they're asked, they say we don't want to be part of Russia, we want to be part of Europe," he said. "And so this is a very major lesson for far right."</p></div><div class="article-body__content"><div class="article-social-share-bottom"><div class="styles_shareActionsContainer__bM9OX" data-testid="share-actions-container"><div class="styles_shareDropdownWrapper__HCkyK" data-activity-map="share-dropdown-article-bottom" data-testid="share-dropdown"><button type="button" class="styles_shareButton__JYLsa" aria-expanded="false" aria-haspopup="true" data-testid="share-dropdown-button"><span class="icon icon-share-drop-icon styles_icon__oYVe4" ","sentiment":"none","content":{"text":["LONDON —  “Europe! Europe!” was the chant ringing out from the banks of the Danube after an election upset that was celebrated across the continent Monday as a stunning defeat for those threatening its security.","Presidents, prime ministers and European Union officials hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity and shared values in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.  ","Orbán, a close ally of Russia's Vladimir Putin and President Donald Trump, is viewed as a trailblazer for the global hard right. He conceded defeat Sunday night in what turned out to be a sweeping victory for challenger Peter Magyar and the center-right Tisza Party, which successfully united the country's opposition behind a vow to restore ties with Europe.","Tisza was set to win 138 seats in the 199-seat parliament, with almost all votes counted.","In a news conference Monday, Magyar offered pragmatic support for the United Nations and the European Union, a huge shift in Hungary's position towards Brussels.","\"We are not going there to fight for the sake of fighting so we can write on billboards that Brussels is evil and needs to be stopped,\" he told supporters in a direct swipe at Orbán's negative campaigning, which also painted Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a potential enemy.","Magyar, who added that it was clear that Ukraine was the \"victim\" in the war, also accused the outgoing government of shredding documents relating to sanctions against Russia, citing an \"insider\" at the foreign ministry. ","A succession of leaders called Magyar to offer their congratulations even before he took to the stage to address large crowds in the capital, Budapest, to declare victory.","Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk wrote on X: “Hungary, Poland, Europe, Back together! Glorious victory, dear friends!” He added “Ruszkik haza,” meaning “Russians go home” in Hungarian, a reference to Orban's backing for Putin and repeated veto on European support for Ukraine against Russia's invasion.","In a video clip posted by Tusk, the clearly delighted Polish leader is seen speaking to Magyar by phone and says \"I think I'm happier than you.\"","Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke for many on the continent when he posted: \"Today Europe wins and European values win.\"","The European Democratic Party, a pan-European centrist group that supports greater integration across the continent, called Magyar's victory \"a cry that shakes the conscience of us all\" and posted a clip of crowds chanting \"Europe! Europe!\" at his victory rally in Budapest.","Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain — no longer a member of the E.U. but a close supporter of Ukraine — said: \"This is an historic moment, not only for Hungary, but for European democracy.\"","The Kremlin, for its part, said Monday it hoped to continue its \"pragmatic\" ties with Hungary. “The Hungarians have made their choice. We respect this choice,\" spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.","But asked later whether the Kremlin would congratulate Magyar, Peskov said “We don’t extend greetings to unfriendly countries. And Hungary is an unfriendly country.\"","Those celebrating the fall of Orbán should be mindful, analysts cautioned, that the huge influence he and his supporters built across the country’s institutions will not be easily removed.","“The structure of the last 16 years has been so entrenched and so much concerned with capturing all the commanding heights of the Hungarian state that it will take quite some time for anyone to unravel this,” said Jonathan Eyal, associated director of the Royal United Services Institute, a think tank in London.","Orbán, 62, is a key European ally of Trump whose populism and culture war posturing has provided a model for the MAGA movement. ","In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces.","The now former Hungarian leader has been widely criticized for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists.","Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. \"We will restore the system of checks and balances,\" Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday. ","He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been halted due to repeated infringements and concerns about corruption and the democratic process.","But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists who have taken power away from Hungary's parliament to make policy, Eyal pointed out, as well as a powerful constitutional court full of his appointees.","\"So sweeping away all these cobwebs is not something that could happen after a party on the Danube River,\" Eyal warned.","Magyar, a relatively young leader at 45, is a former member of Orbán's Fidesz Party and a former official in the Foreign Ministry — he only broke away to join Tisza in 2024.","Magyar is a traditionalist who support the hardline stance of the previous administration on immigration and has even signaled he may go further, by scrapping the country's guest worker scheme for non-E.U. citizens.","\"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader,\" Eyal said. \"So there is, there is a legitimate question about how much of a break this is from someone who clearly shared some of the Orbán government's views for a lot of his political career.\"","And there is unlikely to be a complete severing of ties with Moscow. At his press conference on Monday, Magyar said he hoped the war in Ukraine would soon end so international sanctions on Russia can be lifted — Hungary is a major importer of Russian oil. Hungary will try to diversify, he said, but added that the country can't \"change geography.\" ","Whatever happens, this result is blow to the regime in Moscow, Eyal added.","\"Most Hungarian voters when they're asked, they say we don't want to be part of Russia, we want to be part of Europe,\" he said. \"And so this is a very major lesson for far right.\""]},"documentTracing":{"currentMessageId":{"id":"f8019359-38ac-4463-8016-e6bd314e14bc","updatedDate":"Mon Apr 13 2026 13:46:17 GMT+0000 ","elementId":"b07b58","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces.","elementId":"44dc58","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"The now former Hungarian leader has been\u003ca href=\"https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/viktor-orban-cpac-trump-gop-hungary-leader-rcna40199\" target=\"_blank\"\u003e widely criticized\u003c/a\u003e for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists.","elementId":"54e593","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. \"We will restore the system of checks and balances,\" Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday. ","elementId":"66f141","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been \u003ca href=\"https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/european-union-battling-poland-hungary-illiberal-policies-rcna2888\" target=\"_blank\"\u003ehalted due to repeated infringements \u003c/a\u003eand concerns about corruption and the democratic process.","elementId":"a5c9bd","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists citizens.","elementId":"5277d0","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"\"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader,\" Eyal career.\"","elementId":"c2c580","dataAttributes":[]},{"type":"markup","element":"p","html":"And there is unlikely to be a complete severing of ties with Moscow. gold_text:
Europe celebrates as Orbán's stunning defeat deals a blow to Putin and Trump
Leaders across the continent hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.

LONDON — “Europe! Europe!” was the chant ringing out from the banks of the Danube after an election upset that was celebrated across the continent Monday as a stunning defeat for those threatening its security.

Presidents, prime ministers and European Union officials hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity and shared values in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.

Orbán, a close ally of Russia's Vladimir Putin and President Donald Trump, is viewed as a trailblazer for the global hard right. He conceded defeat Sunday night in what turned out to be a sweeping victory for challenger Peter Magyar and the center-right Tisza Party, which successfully united the country's opposition behind a vow to restore ties with Europe.

Tisza was set to win 138 seats in the 199-seat parliament, with almost all votes counted.

In a news conference Monday, Magyar offered pragmatic support for the United Nations and the European Union, a huge shift in Hungary's position towards Brussels.

"We are not going there to fight for the sake of fighting so we can write on billboards that Brussels is evil and needs to be stopped," he told supporters in a direct swipe at Orbán's negative campaigning, which also painted Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a potential enemy.

Magyar, who added that it was clear that Ukraine was the "victim" in the war, also accused the outgoing government of shredding documents relating to sanctions against Russia, citing an "insider" at the foreign ministry.

A succession of leaders called Magyar to offer their congratulations even before he took to the stage to address large crowds in the capital, Budapest, to declare victory.

Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk wrote on X: “Hungary, Poland, Europe, Back together! Glorious victory, dear friends!” He added “Ruszkik haza,” meaning “Russians go home” in Hungarian, a reference to Orban's backing for Putin and repeated veto on European support for Ukraine against Russia's invasion.

In a video clip posted by Tusk, the clearly delighted Polish leader is seen speaking to Magyar by phone and says "I think I'm happier than you."

Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke for many on the continent when he posted: "Today Europe wins and European values win."

The European Democratic Party, a pan-European centrist group that supports greater integration across the continent, called Magyar's victory "a cry that shakes the conscience of us all" and posted a clip of crowds chanting "Europe! Europe!" at his victory rally in Budapest.

Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain — no longer a member of the E.U. but a close supporter of Ukraine — said: "This is an historic moment, not only for Hungary, but for European democracy."

The Kremlin, for its part, said Monday it hoped to continue its "pragmatic" ties with Hungary. “The Hungarians have made their choice. We respect this choice," spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.

But asked later whether the Kremlin would congratulate Magyar, Peskov said “We don’t extend greetings to unfriendly countries. And Hungary is an unfriendly country."

Those celebrating the fall of Orbán should be mindful, analysts cautioned, that the huge influence he and his supporters built across the country’s institutions will not be easily removed.

“The structure of the last 16 years has been so entrenched and so much concerned with capturing all the commanding heights of the Hungarian state that it will take quite some time for anyone to unravel this,” said Jonathan Eyal, associated director of the Royal United Services Institute, a think tank in London.

Orbán, 62, is a key European ally of Trump whose populism and culture war posturing has provided a model for the MAGA movement.

In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces.

The now former Hungarian leader has been widely criticized for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists.

Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. "We will restore the system of checks and balances," Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday.

He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been halted due to repeated infringements and concerns about corruption and the democratic process.

But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists who have taken power away from Hungary's parliament to make policy, Eyal pointed out, as well as a powerful constitutional court full of his appointees.

"So sweeping away all these cobwebs is not something that could happen after a party on the Danube River," Eyal warned.

Magyar, a relatively young leader at 45, is a former member of Orbán's Fidesz Party and a former official in the Foreign Ministry — he only broke away to join Tisza in 2024.

Magyar is a traditionalist who support the hardline stance of the previous administration on immigration and has even signaled he may go further, by scrapping the country's guest worker scheme for non-E.U. citizens.

"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader," Eyal said. "So there is, there is a legitimate question about how much of a break this is from someone who clearly shared some of the Orbán government's views for a lot of his political career."

And there is unlikely to be a complete severing of ties with Moscow. At his press conference on Monday, Magyar said he hoped the war in Ukraine would soon end so international sanctions on Russia can be lifted — Hungary is a major importer of Russian oil. Hungary will try to diversify, he said, but added that the country can't "change geography."

Whatever happens, this result is blow to the regime in Moscow, Eyal added.

"Most Hungarian voters when they're asked, they say we don't want to be part of Russia, we want to be part of Europe," he said. "And so this is a very major lesson for far right."
